commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=8c391ff3f1e789c2d1efa088b39a2966043c1b7c)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes cron model-provider preflight to resolve request auth headers through the shared provider request config path, pass agent/workspace dirs to credential lookup, redact preflight errors, and add related tests.

PR surface: Source +80, Tests +1045. Total +1125 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source: current main preflight sends a bare GET for local OpenAI-compatible providers, so auth-required local providers can fail before the cron run starts. I did not run a real local provider endpoint in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human handling is needed because the PR has a concrete header-parity defect and still needs contributor-side real behavior proof that automation cannot supply.

target.title: fix(cron): add Authorization header to provider preflight probe
target.body:
## Summary

Replaced the hand-rolled Authorization header logic in the cron preflight probe with the shared `resolveProviderRequestHeaders()` from `provider-request-config.ts`. This ensures the preflight and normal model request paths build identical auth headers for the same config.

## Changes

### P1: Reuse shared auth normalization (ClawSweeper finding)

- **model-preflight.runtime.ts**: Replaced the manual `if (params.apiKey) { headers["Authorization"] = ... }` / custom-header construction with delegation to `resolveProviderRequestHeaders()` via `sanitizeConfiguredProviderRequest()`
- Auth is now resolved through the same code path as normal model requests â€” any future changes to auth formatting apply automatically
- Full credential chain preserved: `request.auth` override â†’ `resolveApiKeyForProvider` fallback â†’ no auth

### P0: Error redaction at cache-write time

- **model-preflight.runtime.ts**: Applied `formatErrorMessage()` to errors before storing in `preflightCache`, preventing unredacted error objects from remaining in heap between cache write and read

### P0: Header prefix trimming consistency

- Prefix is now trimmed via shared `sanitizeConfiguredProviderRequest`, matching the behavior of `resolveAuthOverride` in the normal request path

### P0: Always delegate to resolveProviderRequestHeaders (even without auth)

- The preflight now calls `resolveProviderRequestHeaders` unconditionally, not only when `requestOverrides?.auth` exists
- This ensures `request.headers` are always passed through to the shared header resolver, matching the normal model request path

### P2: Cross-path auth header parity tests

- **model-preflight.runtime.test.ts**: Added 4 parity tests (P0) verifying preflight and normal path produce identical auth headers for the same config
- Added 4 edge case tests (P1): empty header fields, unknown mode, whitespace token, OAuth marker filtering
- Total: 31 tests pass

## Files Changed

| File | Î” |
|------|---|
| `src/cron/isolated-agent/model-preflight.runtime.ts` | +140/-40 |
| `src/cron/isolated-agent/model-preflight.runtime.test.ts` | +445/-3 |

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Cron job preflight was building custom auth headers (`request.auth.mode === "header"`) with hand-rolled logic that missed prefix trimming, header collision cleanup, and `request.headers` merging â€” inconsistent with the shared auth normalization used by normal model requests.

**Real environment tested:**
- **OS:** Ubuntu 24.04 (Oracle VPS)
- **Runtime:** Node.js v24.14.0
- **OpenClaw version:** 2026.5.18 (npm install)
- **Provider:** LiteLLM proxy at http://127.0.0.1:4000
- **Auth method:** LITELLM_API_KEY environment variable

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
```bash
node -e "const m = require('./dist/index.cjs'); m.probeCronModelProvider({ provider: 'litellm', model: 'gpt-4o' }).then(r => console.log(JSON.stringify(r)))"
```

**Evidence after fix:** LiteLLM proxy logs before (top) and after (bottom) the patch show GET /models now gets 200 OK instead of 401:

![Before/after terminal comparison](https://github.com/samson1357924/openclaw/releases/download/evd-pr83648/evidence-pr-img.jpg)

Before â€” no auth header, cron preflight triggers 401:
```
LiteLLM Proxy:ERROR - Exception occured - No api key passed in.
```

After â€” auth header sent, preflight succeeds:
```
127.0.0.1:45914 - "GET /models HTTP/1.1" 200 OK
```

**Observed result after fix:** GET /models response changed from 401 Unauthorized to 200 OK. All 31 preflight unit tests pass, all 30 provider-request-config regression tests pass. Code review confirmed: no Critical or Important issues, auth delegation is correct with proper fallback semantics and edge case handling.

**What was not tested:**
- Integration test with actual local provider endpoint (preflight tests mock `fetchWithSsrFGuard`)
- `prepareProviderRuntimeAuth` plugin auth exchange path (documented gap â€” preflight intentionally skips this for local provider probes)
- Provider-level headers (`providerConfig.headers`) â€” these require SecretInput resolution not available at preflight's raw config stage
target.author: samson1357924

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
